concerns.text: T015 mandates generating synthetic transient-absorption data to 'mock laser flash photolysis' with 'no inherent correlation'. This violates FR-002 ('System MUST capture transient-absorption spectroscopy data') and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which requires results to be reproducible by re-running code against project data. Mocking the primary data source for a research claim about 'real' kinetic lifetimes is a silent relaxation of the requirement to measure actual experimental data.

T029 only implements parsing for implicit solvent models (SMD/PCM). FR-005 explicitly requires the system to accept computed solvation energies from 'explicit solvent models (QM/MM or cluster-continuum) for ≥20% of conditions'. The task list omits any implementation for generating or integrating explicit solvent model data, leaving a mandatory functional requirement unimplemented.

T030 implements linear regression and bootstrapped CIs but omits the 'statistical significance test' and 'multiple-comparison correction' explicitly required by US-3 Acceptance Scenario 1 and SC-003. While the Plan suggests avoiding p-values, the Task must satisfy the Spec's acceptance criteria. The task description fails to include the ANOVA or multiple-comparison correction logic mandated by FR-006 and SC-003.

T034 generates output containing 'p-values' without explicitly framing them as associational diagnostics only. SC-006 mandates 'All findings framed as associational (not causal)'. Generating p-values in the primary output without a specific task step to enforce the 'associational' framing in the metadata or report text risks violating the constitutional requirement to avoid causal claims.
